items.title: Prime Minister Narendra Modi Inaugurates Multiple Development Projects in Varanasi
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated various development projects worth approximately Rs 6,350 crore in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, on April 29, 2026.
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended a women's conference in Varanasi, where he inaugurated multiple development projects. The projects are expected to boost the city's infrastructure and economy. The event was attended by thousands of people, including women from various parts of the country.
keyPoints: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated projects worth Rs 6,350 crore
The projects include infrastructure development and economic growth initiatives
The event was attended by thousands of people, including women from across the country
The projects are expected to create new job opportunities and stimulate local economy
importantPoints: Full form of the conference is not mentioned, but it is a national-level event
Article 14 of the Indian Constitution ensures equal opportunities for all citizens
Varanasi is a historic city and a major tourist destination in India
The projects are part of the government's efforts to boost economic growth and development in the region
The event highlights the government's focus on women's empowerment and development

items.title: CGST Delhi South Commissionerate Detects Fraudulent Input Tax Credit Claim
items.whyInNews: The CGST Delhi South Commissionerate detected a fraudulent claim of input tax credit (ITC) worth approximately Rs 60.59 crore on April 28, 2026.
items.summary: The CGST Delhi South Commissionerate detected a fraudulent claim of input tax credit (ITC) worth approximately Rs 60.59 crore. The claim was made through fake invoices worth Rs 397.23 crore. One person has been arrested in connection with the case.
keyPoints: CGST Delhi South Commissionerate detected a fraudulent ITC claim worth Rs 60.59 crore
The claim was made through fake invoices worth Rs 397.23 crore
One person has been arrested in connection with the case
The detection is part of the government's efforts to prevent tax evasion
importantPoints: Full form of CGST is Central Goods and Services Tax, established in 2017, with headquarters in New Delhi
The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Act, 2017, is the relevant legislation
The detection highlights the government's efforts to prevent tax evasion and ensure compliance
The case is an example of the importance of tax compliance and the consequences of tax evasion
The government has implemented various measures to prevent tax evasion and ensure compliance

items.title: Defence Minister Holds Bilateral Meetings with Counterparts from Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, and Belarus
items.whyInNews: Defence Minister held bilateral meetings with his counterparts from Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, and Belarus in Bishkek on April 27, 2026.
items.summary: Defence Minister held bilateral meetings with his counterparts from Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, and Belarus to discuss defence cooperation and strengthen ties. The meetings were held in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan, and aimed to enhance defence cooperation between the countries.

items.title: All India Institute of Ayurveda Signs MoU with Dr. Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University
items.whyInNews: The All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Dr. Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University on April 26, 2026.
items.summary: The All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Dr. Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University to promote Ayurveda education and research. The MoU aims to enhance cooperation between the two institutions and promote Ayurveda in the country.

items.title: CCI Approves Acquisition of Maple IM, Maple PM, and Maple Trust by MAIF Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd.
items.whyInNews: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) approved the acquisition of Maple IM, Maple PM, and Maple Trust by MAIF Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. on April 25, 2026.

items.title: India Achieves Highest Ever Power Demand of 256 GW
items.whyInNews: India achieved its highest ever power demand of 256 GW on April 28, 2026, without any power cuts.
items.summary: India achieved its highest ever power demand of 256 GW on April 28, 2026, without any power cuts. The achievement highlights the country's ability to meet its growing power demands and ensure energy security.

items.title: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology, Dr. Jitendra Singh, Lays Foundation Stone for New Building of Indian Institute of Astrophysics
items.whyInNews: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology, Dr. Jitendra Singh, laid the foundation stone for the new building of the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA) on April 27, 2026.
items.summary: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology, Dr. Jitendra Singh, laid the foundation stone for the new building of the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA). The new building will be a seven-storeyed structure with a total area of 1 lakh square feet and will be built at a cost of Rs 75 crore.
keyPoints: Dr. Jitendra Singh laid the foundation stone for the new building of IIA
The new building will be a seven-storeyed structure with a total area of 1 lakh square feet

The building will be equipped with state-of-the-art facilities

items.title: National Family Health Survey (NFHS) 6 Data Likely to be Released in May

items.summary: The National Family Health Survey (NFHS) 6 data is likely to be released in May 2026. The survey provides data on various health and family welfare indicators, including fertility, mortality, and nutrition.

items.title: PM Modi Inaugurates Six BCCI Indoor Cricket Academies
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated six BCCI indoor cricket academies across northeast India on April 29, 2026. The academies are located in Rangpo, Doimukh, Imphal, Madankurklang, Aizawl, and Dimapur.
items.summary: The six BCCI indoor cricket academies were inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi to promote cricket in northeast India. The academies will provide world-class training facilities to young cricketers in the region. The inauguration ceremony was attended by BCCI officials and local dignitaries, including the Chief Ministers of the respective states.
keyPoints: Six BCCI indoor cricket academies inaugurated across northeast India
Academies located in Rangpo, Doimukh, Imphal, Madankurklang, Aizawl, and Dimapur
Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the academies on April 29, 2026
Academies will provide world-class training facilities to young cricketers
importantPoints: Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) - founded in 1928, headquarters in Mumbai
Article 19(1)(g) of the Indian Constitution - Right to Practice Any Profession or to Carry on Any Occupation, Trade or Business
First time that BCCI has established indoor cricket academies in northeast India
India ranked 2nd in the ICC Test Championship rankings, with 120 points
The inauguration of the academies is expected to boost cricket development in the region

items.title: Indian TT Team Gets ₹90 Lakh Support
items.whyInNews: The Indian table tennis team has received ₹90 lakh support from the Sports Authority of India (SAI) ahead of the centenary World Championships. The team will participate in the championships, which will be held from April 30 to May 7, 2026.
items.summary: The Indian table tennis team has received ₹90 lakh support from the Sports Authority of India (SAI) to participate in the centenary World Championships. The team will undergo a training camp in Germany before the championships. The SAI has also provided the team with three coaches, three support staff, and three sparring partners.
keyPoints: Indian table tennis team receives ₹90 lakh support from SAI
Team will participate in the centenary World Championships from April 30 to May 7, 2026

importantPoints: Sports Authority of India (SAI) - founded in 1982, headquarters in New Delhi

India ranked 24th in the ITTF World Team Rankings, with 120 points
The support from SAI is expected to boost the team's performance in the championships

items.title: Virat Kohli Becomes First Batter to Complete 9000 Runs in IPL
items.whyInNews: Virat Kohli has become the first batter to complete 9000 runs in the Indian Premier League (IPL). He achieved this milestone while playing for the Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) against the Delhi Capitals on April 28, 2026.
items.summary: Virat Kohli has become the first batter to complete 9000 runs in the Indian Premier League (IPL). He achieved this milestone while playing for the Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) against the Delhi Capitals on April 28, 2026. Kohli has been one of the most consistent performers in the IPL, with an average of 40.45 and a strike rate of 129.94.
keyPoints: Virat Kohli becomes first batter to complete 9000 runs in IPL
Kohli achieved this milestone while playing for RCB against Delhi Capitals on April 28, 2026
Kohli has an average of 40.45 and a strike rate of 129.94 in the IPL
Kohli has played for RCB since the inception of the IPL in 2008
importantPoints: Indian Premier League (IPL) - founded in 2008, headquarters in Mumbai
Article 19(1)(g) of the Indian Constitution - Right to Practice Any Profession or to Carry on Any Occupation, Trade or Business
First time that a batter has completed 9000 runs in the IPL
Kohli ranked 2nd in the IPL all-time leading run-scorers list, with 9000 runs
The achievement is a testament to Kohli's consistency and skill in the IPL
